summary: IAM user 'svc-scanner' performed read-only EC2 and STS reconnaissance (DescribeInstances, DescribeSecurityGroups, GetCallerIdentity) and attempted ListBuckets — all via confirmed Tor exit node 198.51.100.77, which has 12 abuse reports in 90 days. The 72-hour CloudTrail baseline shows this account normally calls DescribeInstances at ~02:00 UTC from a fixed IP (192.0.2.140), with no prior Tor use, indicating a clear behavioural deviation. However, the account name 'svc-scanner' and exclusively read-only API calls are consistent with authorised pentest or audit tooling, and an intentional Tor-sourced assessment cannot be ruled out without human verification.

evidence.observation: Recon:IAMUser/TorIPCaller — svc-scanner called DescribeInstances (×9), DescribeSecurityGroups (×4), GetCallerIdentity (×1) from Tor exit node 198.51.100.77 (ASN 64499, EXAMPLE-RELAYNET, Frankfurt) in a 13-minute window; 14 events total.
198.51.100.77 confirmed as Tor exit node; reputation score 55; tagged ['tor','anonymiser']; 12 abuse reports in 90 days; active 2025-11-19 to 2026-07-13 (today).

All four Tor-sourced events occurred 07:52–07:58 UTC on 2026-07-13; baseline shows DescribeInstances at 02:00 UTC on 2026-07-11 and 2026-07-12 from fixed IP 192.0.2.140 — no prior Tor usage in 72 hours, and ListBuckets (AccessDenied) adds scope not seen in baseline.
